mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached, or signal IC is negative over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the industrial production index during late-cycle expansions indicate supply chain bottlenecks and profit margin compression, causing immediate negative equity price adjustments.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of late-cycle industrial production contractions, or the signal loses predictive power when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation falling below 1.5% annually triggers expectations of aggressive central bank easing, which compresses yield curves and generates positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets decline over the subsequent 20 days in >55% of low-inflation observations, or the inflation threshold shows no statistical edge in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and corporate revenue projections, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months following payroll growth, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between stagnant GDP growth and accelerating retail sales indicates unsustainable consumer leverage that precedes sharp earnings downgrades and immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative 5-day returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial output combined with stable housing construction during early recovery phases signals improving capacity utilization and margin expansion, driving positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in <45% of early-cycle industrial production expansions, or the mechanism shows negative IC when real interest rates exceed 2.5%.
